stem: A factory produces solar panels using two sources. Automated Line East handles 58% of production with a known defect rate of 3.6%. Automated Line West handles 42% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 5.0% and 7.9%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected solar panel is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Automated Line West?

ground_truth.answer: P(Automated Line West | defective) ranges from 0.501 to 0.614 as Automated Line West's defect rate varies from 5.0% to 7.9%. Point estimate at 6.5%: 0.567.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.501, 0.614] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
